title: Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled
description: Experience the iconic flavors of India with this authentic Tandoori Chicken, marinated in a rich, spiced yogurt and slow-grilled over charcoal. Each succulent piece boasts a smoky char, tender interior, and a vibrant burst of aromatic spices, making it a beloved centerpiece for any Indian dinner.

ingredients.item: Chicken (whole, skin removed, cut into 8 pieces, or 4-6 large leg quarters)

ingredients.notes: For vibrant color and mild heat, divided for both marinades
Drained of excess water for at least 2 hours

instructions.title: Prepare the Chicken
instructions.text: Thoroughly wash the chicken pieces and pat them completely dry with paper towels. Using a sharp knife, make deep slits (2-3 per piece, depending on size) on the thickest parts of the chicken, ensuring they penetrate to the bone. This helps the marinade penetrate deeply.

instructions.title: First Marination
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with lemon juice, 1 tsp salt, and 1/2 tbsp Kashmiri red chili powder. Rub this mixture vigorously into the chicken, making sure to get it into the slits. Cover the bowl and let it marinate at room temperature for 30 minutes. This step helps tenderize the chicken and build a foundational layer of flavor.

instructions.title: Second Marination
instructions.text: While the first marinade is working, prepare the second marinade. In another bowl, combine the hung curd, ginger-garlic paste, remaining 1 tbsp Kashmiri red chili powder, turmeric powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, garam masala, crushed kasuri methi, black salt, and optional red food color. Add the heated and cooled mustard oil and mix everything thoroughly until well combined. Drain any liquid from the first-marinated chicken, then add it to the second marinade. Rub the marinade generously all over the chicken, pushing it into the slits. Cover and refrigerate for a minimum of 6-8 hours, or preferably overnight, for best results.

instructions.title: Prepare the Charcoal Grill
instructions.text: About 45 minutes before grilling, light your charcoal. Arrange the charcoal to create medium-hot zones. The ideal temperature is when the coals are glowing red and covered with a light layer of ash. Clean the grill grates thoroughly and lightly oil them to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Charcoal Grilling
instructions.text: Remove the chicken from the refrigerator 30 minutes before grilling to bring it to room temperature. Place the marinated chicken pieces directly on the prepared grill grates over medium-hot coals. Grill for about 20-25 minutes, turning every 4-5 minutes to ensure even cooking and charring on all sides. During the last 10 minutes of grilling, baste the chicken generously with melted butter or ghee. Continue grilling until the chicken is cooked through, juices run clear, and it has developed a beautiful smoky char.

instructions.title: Rest and Serve
instructions.text: Once cooked, remove the Tandoori Chicken from the grill and let it rest for 5 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute, keeping the chicken moist and flavorful. Sprinkle generously with chaat masala and serve hot with fresh lemon wedges, mint chutney, and laccha pyaaz (sliced onions).

tips: **Hung Curd is Key:** Ensure your yogurt is properly drained (hung) to remove excess water. This creates a thick marinade that clings to the chicken, preventing it from becoming watery on the grill.
**Deep Slits Matter:** Don't be shy with the knife! Deep slits allow the marinades to penetrate to the bone, infusing flavor and tenderizing the chicken from within.
**Mustard Oil's Role:** Heating and cooling the mustard oil before adding it to the marinade mellows its pungent flavor while still imparting its unique, authentic taste to the Tandoori Chicken.

**Don't Overcrowd the Grill:** Grill in batches if necessary. Overcrowding lowers the grill temperature and steams the chicken instead of charring it, preventing that desired smoky flavor and crispy exterior.
article:
## History of Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled

Tandoori Chicken, an iconic dish of Indian cuisine, has a relatively modern origin, though its roots are ancient. The concept of cooking in a tandoor (a cylindrical clay oven) dates back thousands of years in the Indian subcontinent. However, the Tandoori Chicken as we know it today, characterized by its vibrant red hue and smoky flavor, was popularized in the 1940s by Kundan Lal Gujral at his Moti Mahal restaurant in Peshawar (pre-partition India) and later in Delhi. Gujral is credited with inventing the modern version of Tandoori Chicken, along with Butter Chicken and Dal Makhani, adapting traditional tandoor cooking to create dishes that became globally famous. The dish truly took off after India's partition, becoming a staple in North Indian restaurants and a symbol of Indian culinary identity worldwide. Its preparation over charcoal in traditional tandoors or modern grills continues to be a cherished technique.

## Why This Recipe Works

This recipe for Tandoori Chicken works exceptionally well due to the synergy of its authentic ingredients and meticulous preparation. The two-stage marination process is crucial: the first, with lemon juice and salt, tenderizes the chicken and allows initial flavors to penetrate. The second, a rich, thick marinade of hung curd and a complex blend of spices (ginger-garlic, Kashmiri chili, turmeric, cumin, coriander, garam masala, kasuri methi, black salt), infuses deep, layered flavors. The acidity of the yogurt further tenderizes the meat, while the mustard oil helps carry the fat-soluble spices and contributes to the signature taste. Grilling over charcoal imparts an irreplaceable smoky aroma and a beautifully charred exterior, locking in moisture and creating a perfect balance of textures and tastes that is the hallmark of genuine Tandoori Chicken.

## Chef Tips for Perfection

To achieve Tandoori Chicken perfection, several chef's tips are indispensable. Firstly, ensure your chicken has deep cuts; this is not just for aesthetics but for maximum flavor absorption and even cooking. Secondly, the hung curd must be very thick; draining excess water prevents a watery marinade that slides off the chicken and hinders charring. Thirdly, don't rush the marination. Overnight marination in the refrigerator is highly recommended as it allows the flavors to fully develop and the chicken to tenderize beautifully. When grilling, maintain a consistent medium-hot heat and avoid overloading the grill to prevent steaming. Regular turning and basting with butter or ghee during the final stages will create a moist interior and a glossy, flavorful crust. Finally, always allow the cooked chicken to rest for a few minutes before serving to ensure the juices redistribute, resulting in more succulent meat.

## Serving Suggestions

Authentic Tandoori Chicken is best enjoyed as part of a traditional Indian meal. The classic accompaniment is a vibrant green mint-coriander chutney, offering a refreshing counterpoint to the rich spices. Thinly sliced red onions, often marinated lightly with lemon juice and chaat masala (known as laccha pyaaz), add a welcome crunch and tang. For a complete experience, serve it with warm, fluffy naan bread, roti, or paratha, perfect for scooping up the succulent chicken and any leftover marinade. A cooling raita (yogurt dip with cucumber or boondi) can also balance the heat. For a more elaborate dinner, pair it with a simple jeera rice (cumin rice) or a light dal. The vibrant colors and bold flavors of Tandoori Chicken make it an impressive dish for any gathering.

jsonLd.name: Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled
jsonLd.description: Experience the iconic flavors of India with this authentic Tandoori Chicken, marinated in a rich, spiced yogurt and slow-grilled over charcoal. Each succulent piece boasts a smoky char, tender interior, and a vibrant burst of aromatic spices, making it a beloved centerpiece for any Indian dinner.
jsonLd.keywords: Indian, Indian, authentic, traditional, dinner, Indian, Tandoori, Chicken, Charcoal Grilled, authentic, traditional, dinner, spicy, yogurt marinade
recipeIngredient: 1.2 kg Chicken (whole, skin removed, cut into 8 pieces, or 4-6 large leg quarters) (With deep slits)
1.5 tbsp Kashmiri Red Chili Powder (For vibrant color and mild heat, divided for both marinades)
1 cup Hung Curd (thick yogurt) (Drained of excess water for at least 2 hours)
2 tbsp Mustard Oil (Heated and cooled, for marinade)
1/4 tsp Red Food Color (Optional, for authentic restaurant-style color)
3 tbsp Unsalted Butter or Ghee (Melted, for basting)

recipeInstructions.name: Prepare the Chicken
recipeInstructions.text: Thoroughly wash the chicken pieces and pat them completely dry with paper towels. Using a sharp knife, make deep slits (2-3 per piece, depending on size) on the thickest parts of the chicken, ensuring they penetrate to the bone. This helps the marinade penetrate deeply.

recipeInstructions.name: First Marination
recipeInstructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with lemon juice, 1 tsp salt, and 1/2 tbsp Kashmiri red chili powder. Rub this mixture vigorously into the chicken, making sure to get it into the slits. Cover the bowl and let it marinate at room temperature for 30 minutes. This step helps tenderize the chicken and build a foundational layer of flavor.

recipeInstructions.name: Second Marination
recipeInstructions.text: While the first marinade is working, prepare the second marinade. In another bowl, combine the hung curd, ginger-garlic paste, remaining 1 tbsp Kashmiri red chili powder, turmeric powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, garam masala, crushed kasuri methi, black salt, and optional red food color. Add the heated and cooled mustard oil and mix everything thoroughly until well combined. Drain any liquid from the first-marinated chicken, then add it to the second marinade. Rub the marinade generously all over the chicken, pushing it into the slits. Cover and refrigerate for a minimum of 6-8 hours, or preferably overnight, for best results.

recipeInstructions.name: Prepare the Charcoal Grill
recipeInstructions.text: About 45 minutes before grilling, light your charcoal. Arrange the charcoal to create medium-hot zones. The ideal temperature is when the coals are glowing red and covered with a light layer of ash. Clean the grill grates thoroughly and lightly oil them to prevent sticking.

recipeInstructions.name: Charcoal Grilling
recipeInstructions.text: Remove the chicken from the refrigerator 30 minutes before grilling to bring it to room temperature. Place the marinated chicken pieces directly on the prepared grill grates over medium-hot coals. During the last 10 minutes of grilling, baste the chicken generously with melted butter or ghee. Continue grilling until the chicken is cooked through, juices run clear, and it has developed a beautiful smoky char.

recipeInstructions.name: Rest and Serve
recipeInstructions.text: Once cooked, remove the Tandoori Chicken from the grill and let it rest for 5 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute, keeping the chicken moist and flavorful. Sprinkle generously with chaat masala and serve hot with fresh lemon wedges, mint chutney, and laccha pyaaz (sliced onions).
